name: VENTRICULAR SEPTAL DEFECT
definition: small restrictive VSD with defect < 1 cm; little / no hemodynamic significance with PATHOGNOMONIC for pulmonary arterial hypertension

demographics.Frequency: 10% of large VSDs by 2 years of age

dominant_finding: slight prominence of pulmonary vessels (45% shunt)
discriminating_features: slight prominence of pulmonary vessels (45% shunt)
prominent pulmonary segment + vessels (= shunt vascularity)

general: slight prominence of pulmonary vessels (45% shunt)
prominent pulmonary segment + vessels (= shunt vascularity)
eventual decrease of pulmonary vessel caliber
eventual decrease in size of LA + LV
variable appearance ← variations in defect size
increase in pulmonary blood flow (if > 45% of pulmonary blood flow from systemic
prolapse of aortic valve cusp (in supracristal VSD)
deformity of aortic cusp (in membranous VSD)
lack of echoes in region of interventricular septum with sharp edges (DDx: artifactual
ANGIO: RVOT / pulmonary valve fill without filling of RV chamber (in supracristal VSD)

clinical.Cx: right aortic valve cusp may herniate into VSD → aortic insufficiency
